flashcards.back: A compiler is a language processor that translates entire high-level language code into machine code before execution.

flashcards.front: What is the difference between syntax and semantics?
flashcards.back: Syntax refers to the grammatical structure of code, while semantics refers to the meaning or behavior of the code when executed.

flashcards.front: Name the two main phases of compilation.

flashcards.back: An assembler translates assembly language code into machine code.

flashcards.front: What is the role of a lexical analyzer?
flashcards.back: To scan source code and convert sequences of characters into tokens.
